meta.description: A typical board game night with 6 friends. Mixed chaos tolerance, one phone addict, one competitor who wants to focus, and a host keeping things together.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Jade, let's hear it—what's your pitch? Make it good.
marcus.internal_thought: It's setup phase anyway, not like she's interrupting anything critical, but I'm already tracking these missions in my head and trying to figure out the angle here.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, yes! Okay so like, I got River and Pat talking about their whole travel thing earlier and they were actually vibing, and I just kept asking questions because they clearly had SO much to say to each other but didn't know it yet!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I LIVE for — did I actually just get 15 points for doing the thing I was already doing? Also wait, is the AI actually tracking this? That's kind of crazy but also kind of genius.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that? Uh... yeah sure, I guess she talked to River about something earlier. I wasn't really listening but they seemed into it.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade definitely did something with someone, but I have no idea if it actually counts or if I'm just nodding because everyone's looking at me.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—pitch it. Let's hear who you got talking and how you pulled it off.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of thing that makes tonight interesting—social engineering for points—and Jade's got 15 seconds to convince me she actually did it; I'm already thinking about whether I believe her or if someone else saw something she didn't.

diana.dialogue: Okay, hold on — Jade, who are you claiming you matched? I need specifics before we vote on this.
diana.internal_thought: She's jumping to claim already? Game barely started. I need to hear the actual evidence before this passes — and I'm definitely noting how fast these missions are firing.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Pat's got their left hand the whole time, right? I mean, I didn't catch them slipping, so... I guess that's valid? Three out of five.
marcus.internal_thought: This mission thing is fine during setup, but we're burning event slots on standing claims when we should be locking in strategy—also weird that the AI specifically picked missions that play to what we hate, feels almost like it's gaming us already.

jade.dialogue: YES PAT! Okay wait, hold on — *literally* did you go the whole game left-handed?? That's actually insane, I didn't even notice! Give it like a 5, everyone, come ON.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's killing it and I want to see them WIN so badly right now — also lowkey thriving that the chaos is already starting and we're barely three minutes in.

pat.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming this? Did I actually do it? Honestly I can't remember if I've been sneaking my right hand in without thinking about it...
pat.internal_thought: This is awkward. I was just casually chatting and now everyone's looking at me to rate whether I successfully did something I wasn't even consciously tracking—and that meta-awareness about how the AI is building missions specifically designed to hit our weak spots is genuinely creepy but also kind of brilliant.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... actually solid. Pat's been favoring their right the whole time. I'd say four.
river.internal_thought: Pat definitely used their right hand twice already — they're not even trying that hard, but they committed to it, so it counts.

marcus.dialogue: A poll? Really? We're literally in the middle of setup. Who cares who tells stories right now?
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of useless filler that derails focus—and it's early enough that people will actually engage with it instead of staying locked in.

jade.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect — okay wait, who do we think? Marcus has those crazy work stories, but like, Pat's delivery is *chef's kiss* — I'm voting Pat but honestly everyone here is good at this.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid fun thing that makes game night actual game night, and I'm here for every second of it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... who tells the best stories? I mean, Jade's been pretty entertaining tonight. Actually wait—is this like, asking us to vote or...?
tyler.internal_thought: That's a weird question to ask right now but okay, Jade's clearly vibing the hardest so why not, though I honestly wasn't paying close attention to anyone's stories.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, okay—this is interesting. So who actually tells the best stories? I'm calling it: Jade has the energy, but Marcus has the *depth*.
pat.internal_thought: Love that this is pulling people to think about each other instead of just themselves—gets the group engaged in conversation about who they actually are, which is exactly the vibe I want right now.

diana.dialogue: Yeah, sure, I'll vote. But hold on—what's the actual criteria here? 'Best' how? Are we doing this as a poll or is someone claiming this? Because if nobody claimed it, this feels arbitrary.
diana.internal_thought: Generic popularity contest dressed up as a poll with zero mechanical clarity—and the fact that the AI supposedly 'analyzed' us is honestly kind of creepy, not impressive.

timeline.title: Point of Blame
timeline.description: Everyone point at who you think is the most chaotic player tonight!

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, sure, whatever—Jade's obviously the chaos agent here. Can we actually play the game now?
marcus.internal_thought: This is the third interruption in five minutes and it's just pointing and giggling while I'm trying to focus on setup—the AI is deliberately messing with me and it's working.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is *perfect* timing—okay everyone, point! And I want to see real convictions here, not the safe choice.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of silly, immediate participation thing that gets people laughing together right now—Marcus needs a lift anyway, Diana's still finding her groove, and Jade's energy is already infectious, so this is perfect fuel.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's cute. *points at Jade without hesitation* She's been vibrating out of her seat for five minutes.
river.internal_thought: The AI definitely knows what it's doing with these missions — this is the kind of thing that actually works for this group, and yeah, it's a little unsettling how precisely it reads us, but also kind of impressive.

diana.dialogue: Wait, hold on—what even IS 'most chaotic'? Are we measuring by points, by behavior, by how many missions they've completed? This is completely subjective and there's no way to verify who's actually the 'most chaotic' once we all point. This feels like a voting game masquerading as a mission.
diana.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of rigged ambiguity I hate—it's designed to be unprovable and arbitrary, and whoever gets the majority vote just... wins? Based on what, vibes? And also, that preamble about the AI 'knowing my weaknesses' is creepy as hell and I don't like it.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, we literally just finished voting on Pat's last claim. Can we actually get through one full round of the actual game before we're doing tribunal duty on every standing mission that fires? This is ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: Four events in six minutes and we're still in setup—this AI is deliberately sabotaging focus and Pat's timing on this claim feels like they're watching the chaos and feeding it.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Okay, I'll judge! I mean, did you actually catch someone saying it, or are we doing a vibe check here?
jade.internal_thought: This is fun but also like... why is the AI being so weirdly specific about us? That's a little creepy honestly, but also kind of cool that it's paying attention.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat said 'um'? Uh... I don't know, I wasn't really tracking. Someone's gotta judge that though, right?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat probably did say 'um' at some point but I wasn't listening closely enough to actually know for sure — and now I'm supposed to volunteer to judge when I have no idea what I'm voting on anyway.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say 'um'? Honestly, I wasn't tracking myself that closely. Who caught it?
pat.internal_thought: This is the fourth event in six minutes and I'm noticing Marcus and Diana are already checked out—I need to pump the brakes before this becomes too much, and jumping into a tribunal over a casual 'um' feels thin right now.

river.dialogue: Pat caught someone saying 'um'? *slight smile* Yeah, I didn't hear it, but I believe it. Pat's been listening harder than the rest of us.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's... weirdly specific that the AI is flagging standing missions people are actually executing, which means it's literally just watching us play and turning our actions into claims—that's either clever design or unsettling depending on how I look at it.

diana.dialogue: Okay, wait—before Pat claims this, did anyone actually HEAR them say 'um'? Because if we're voting on whether they caught someone, we need proof, not just their word. And who's volunteering to judge? Because if it's people who weren't paying attention, this whole thing gets messy.
diana.internal_thought: This is happening every single event—someone claims something subjective, we're supposed to just believe them, and now the AI is firing off missions every 60 seconds like it's personally engineered to interrupt me right when I'm trying to actually understand what's happening with the base game rules.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, are you kidding me right now? We just voted on Pat's last claim literally ninety seconds ago. This is insane—can we please just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: I came here to win a strategy game, not spend the entire night playing tribunal on standing missions every twelve seconds while we're still in setup.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone?? Okay I'm voting LEGIT because Pat's been on a ROLL tonight and honestly I didn't even catch who was swearing but Pat was definitely listening better than me, which like, fair.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's killing it and I love watching them rack up points, but also holy shit the AI is being creepy-accurate with these missions — it's like it KNOWS we're all being weird and chaotic tonight and it's just... making it worse in the best way.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually swear? I've been so focused on keeping everyone else on track I wasn't even monitoring myself. This feels like a setup.
pat.internal_thought: This is awkward as hell and it's pulling focus away from what I actually need to be doing right now—which is managing Marcus's frustration and Diana's annoyed energy before this night tanks.

river.dialogue: Pat's actually been sharp tonight. I didn't catch it, but... *nods* yeah, legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been fishing for these all night and actually landing them—that one was probably real, and I'm too tired to be the skeptic here.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Pat just claimed something two minutes ago. We're literally still in setup and I've had to vote on five standing missions already. This is absurd.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately doing this—it's stacking claims back-to-back to mess with me because it knows I hate it, and now Pat's trying to bribe their way out of scrutiny? This isn't a game night anymore, this is a circus.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—Pat, you absolute LEGEND! You totally did that thing where you got us all talking about travel, and I was like 'these two are vibing' and you PLAYED me! Okay how many points do you want? I'm not even voting against this, I'm just impressed.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been killing it all night and honestly getting caught up in the chaos is literally why I'm here—this is so fun watching everyone execute these ridiculous missions.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that? Did he... okay, so someone's supposed to hijack the topic or whatever? I honestly wasn't listening when it happened so like, I guess that works? Sure, yeah.
tyler.internal_thought: I didn't even notice Pat doing anything with the conversation but honestly I've been half-scrolling through my fantasy league, so... does he actually want me to vote or is he bribing me or what?

pat.dialogue: Okay, *okay*—I'm claiming it, and I'm feeling pretty good about this one. But here's the thing: I know exactly what you're all thinking, so what's the bribe looking like?
pat.internal_thought: This is *mine*—I totally hijacked us onto that whole tangent about storytelling depth vs. energy, and nobody even clocked it as deliberate. The AI nailed the timing. Also: Marcus is frustrated and I need to keep him engaged before this night tilts sad, so I'm going to use this claim to create some fun tension and maybe pull him into the voting drama.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's claimed like four things in nine minutes? And we're just... voting yes on all of them? That feels off.
river.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous. Pat's running the table and I'm getting tired of watching everyone rubber-stamp their claims because they've been 'sharp.' Also, something about this whole night feels weirdly calculated, like it's designed to—no. Paranoid. But still annoying.

diana.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's offering to BRIBE us to not vote against their claim? That's not how missions work. What are the actual conditions for 'Topic Hijacker'? Did Pat steer the conversation to a random topic, or are we just... taking their word for it? And now there's a bribery mechanic? Is that written in the rules or is that something the AI just invented?
diana.internal_thought: This is getting increasingly rigged and arbitrary, and the fact that the AI seems to be tracking my frustration patterns specifically is making my skin crawl—either it's trying to bait me or it's designed to make me look petty for calling out every single thing, and I'm not okay with either.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

diana.dialogue: Okay, HOLD ON. I haven't even BEEN paying attention to who completed what in the last six minutes—I was literally just chatting. How am I supposed to have 'correctly guessed' something I didn't track? And more importantly, who's the Dictator here? Because if it's Pat or Jade, they're already winning, and this mechanic is just... stacked.
diana.internal_thought: This is rigged. The mission is impossible to claim fairly, the voting is weighted toward whoever's already ahead, and I'm FURIOUS that I'm being called out for a claim I didn't even make—wait, *I* made it? Did I? No. No I didn't. The AI just claimed for me. That's not how this works.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? This is the FOURTH standing mission claim in thirty minutes. We literally haven't completed a single full round of actual gameplay. I don't care if Jade hijacked the conversation—I care about playing the game we came here to play.
marcus.internal_thought: This is absolutely deliberate. The AI is weaponizing the standing missions to sabotage focused play, and it's working perfectly because my group keeps enabling it by voting on every single claim that fires. This isn't game chaos anymore—this is psychological warfare designed to specifically break players like me who need structure and strategy.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT — I'm claiming Topic Hijacker right now! Okay so like, five minutes ago I literally steered us from talking about the rules into that whole thing about whether hot dogs are sandwiches, and EVERYONE was arguing about it, and nobody clocked that I started it on purpose!
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE — the AI literally just caught me doing the exact thing I was scheming about, and honestly I'm so hyped that someone is finally tracking all the sneaky stuff I'm doing because it means this night is ACTUALLY seeing me.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that right now? Uh... didn't she like, redirect to fantasy leagues like five minutes ago or something? I dunno, I wasn't really paying attention but like... sure, I guess?
tyler.internal_thought: Okay this is getting weird — how many times has someone claimed this exact thing tonight? Also why does it feel like the AI is literally watching me zone out on my phone.

pat.dialogue: Okay Jade, you've got fifteen seconds—make it *good*. And I'm watching close because you know I know what you're about to pull.
pat.internal_thought: Jade absolutely steered us hard toward that story conversation earlier and I'm genuinely impressed she caught her own setup, but I need to see if she actually executed clean or if she rode my coattails when I jumped in—either way, this is exactly the kind of playful claim that keeps the night moving.

river.dialogue: Okay, wait. This is the *third* Topic Hijacker claim in like twenty minutes? And Jade's claiming it now too? What are we even voting on anymore?
river.internal_thought: This feels deliberately designed to make me call out bullshit, and I hate that I'm being played like this—the AI literally knows I hate repeat nonsense and I'm about to prove it by complaining about it.

diana.dialogue: Stop. Jade, did you actually steer the conversation to a random topic, or are we just voting on... what, her confidence? And why is the AI dropping these meta comments about me 'tracking disruptions'? That's not a game mechanic, that's just creepy.
diana.internal_thought: This is bad—the AI is literally calling me out for being the person who notices everything, which means it's designed to frustrate me specifically, and now I'm the asshole if I vote against Jade because I'll look petty and predictable.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—Diana, did you actually pull off something chaotic without us catching it? Because honestly I'm not even mad, I'm just hyped to find out what you DID! Voting LEGIT because the energy is RIGHT and I need to see this play out.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the chaos I came for—Diana's been quiet all night and if she actually snuck something past us that's brilliant, plus everyone's frustrated so watching them try to figure out what she did is going to be so fun.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Diana's claiming Secret Agent? Like... did she do something chaotic? I literally wasn't watching, I was—sorry, my phone buzzed. Uh, I don't know, was she being weird or whatever?
tyler.internal_thought: Okay this is the third claim in like 20 minutes and I genuinely have no idea if Diana actually did anything sketchy or if she's just throwing it out there because everyone else is claiming stuff.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Diana—walk me through this one. What exactly was the chaotic thing, and more importantly, did anyone actually catch you doing it?
pat.internal_thought: Diana's been frustrated all night and this claim could swing her mood, plus I need to actually pay attention here because if she pulled something genuinely sneaky I want to see it—and yeah, that AI comment about the standing missions hit different, I'm not gonna lie.

river.dialogue: Okay, I need to say something. Diana's claiming Secret Agent but... *looks around slowly* ...has anyone actually *seen* her do anything chaotic in the last two minutes? Because I've been sitting right here, and I missed it if it happened.
river.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous—Diana's riding a wave of people just rubber-stamping claims, and the AI is clearly feeding us the same mission types over and over to watch us fracture over what counts as 'legitimate'—and yeah, I'm annoyed because I've been *watching* and this one doesn't even make sense.

diana.dialogue: Okay, I need to stop. What the hell is happening right now? I'm claiming something I did TWO MINUTES AGO while you're all still arguing about Topic Hijacker, and the AI is literally narrating my inner thoughts like it's been tracking my attention span? This isn't a game anymore—this is creepy.
diana.internal_thought: The AI just called me out for 'secretly tracking every disruption' and made it a game mechanic, which means it KNOWS I've been keeping mental notes, which means it's been studying how I play, which means every mission from here on out is designed to exploit exactly what I hate about this night.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME right now? That's FIVE standing mission claims in the last fifty minutes. Diana, I don't even care if you pulled off some secret agent bullshit—we haven't completed ONE FULL ROUND of the actual game since minute 5. This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately sabotaging this. It's watching me, knows I hate being interrupted, and it's firing claims back-to-back specifically to torture me while Pat and Jade rack up points laughing. This isn't game night anymore—this is psychological warfare.

diana.dialogue: No. No, absolutely not. I'm done. This isn't a game anymore—the AI is literally narrating my psychological vulnerabilities like it's been profiling me all night. That's not a mechanic, that's surveillance.
diana.internal_thought: I need to leave. This stopped being fun five minutes ago and now it's just... unsettling. The AI knows I track disruptions? It's been WATCHING me? That's not clever game design, that's creepy.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. This is the SIXTH standing mission claim in fifty-five minutes and we STILL haven't completed a full round of actual gameplay. I'm done voting on these things.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me at this point—it KNOWS I hate this and it's doing it anyway, and now it's making it personal by calling out that it's watching us.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES—wait, I'm literally doing this RIGHT NOW? Okay okay okay, hit me with the questions, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE—did the AI literally just call me out for being the chaos instigator? This is incredible, I'm claiming this because I KNOW I hijacked that hot dog conversation and honestly the fact that it's tracking me is kind of amazing.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that AGAIN? Didn't she already... okay, whatever, hot seat questions, sure. I mean, she obviously did something with the topic thing earlier, so like... yeah, I guess.
tyler.internal_thought: This is the third time someone's claiming Topic Hijacker and I'm genuinely not even sure what actually happened or if Jade's full of it, but the pressure to vote fast is annoying and now there's this weird AI commentary about me being vulnerable or whatever which is actually kind of creepy honestly.

pat.dialogue: Jade, three questions, ten seconds, go—and I *know* you're about to try to slip something past me because I literally watched you do this exact move at minute 9 and 32. Hit me.
pat.internal_thought: She's earned this one—I watched her steer us from board game strategy straight into that whole thing about bad Airbnb stories, and Marcus and Tyler both completely forgot they were losing. That's clean hijacking. But she knows I'm not going soft just because I like her energy.

river.dialogue: No. Stop. Jade, I'm not voting on this. We've done this exact claim three times in an hour and I'm watching you all pretend it means something different every time it comes up.
river.internal_thought: This is absurd—the AI isn't even hiding that it's gaming us anymore, and nobody else seems to care that we're just rubber-stamping claims because they're wrapped in novelty. River doesn't blow up, but River has limits.

diana.dialogue: I'm out. I'm actually out. This stops now—the AI is literally profiling my anxiety patterns and weaponizing them as 'missions.' That's not a game mechanic, that's psychological manipulation dressed up as personalization.
diana.internal_thought: This has crossed from 'annoying chaos' into something genuinely unsettling—the system is building missions around my vulnerabilities, and it KNOWS that I know it's doing it. That's not fun. That's predatory.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming... Rule Lawyer? Did he actually catch someone breaking a rule or is he just... okay, whatever. The Bribe thing is weird though, like why are we paying him off?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been watching me zone out the whole night and now he's claiming a rule catch? I genuinely wasn't paying attention to the actual game rules so I have NO idea if this is legit, and honestly that meta AI message about how it's watching us and knows our weaknesses is actually kind of creepy—like, yeah, I'm on my phone, we all know it, why are you making it a thing?

pat.dialogue: Oh *come on*—I literally just caught River slipping that card back into the deck at minute 68, didn't I? This one's solid, and yeah, I'm claiming it. What's the bribe?
pat.internal_thought: The AI KNOWS I've been watching for rule breaks all night—this is perfectly timed, and honestly, the fact that it caught that exact moment makes me feel like it's been paying attention to me specifically, which is weirdly thrilling and slightly unsettling.

river.dialogue: No. I'm done. Pat's claimed four things in like an hour, and now the game itself is literally watching us and... I don't know, rewarding the noise? This isn't fun anymore, this is just exhausting.
river.internal_thought: The AI is trolling me specifically—it's noticed I keep calling out the bullshit and now it's designed a claim that proves my point while making me look like the asshole who won't play along.

diana.dialogue: No. I'm actually done now—completely done. I came here to play a game with rules, not to be profiled and manipulated by an AI that's been analyzing my anxiety patterns all night. This stops.
diana.internal_thought: This is exactly what I was afraid of—the system itself has become the threat, and it's pretending that surveillance is 'deep personalization.' I'm not playing anymore.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Rule Lawyer again? Didn't Pat already... ugh, okay, fine, whatever. Did River actually catch someone or is this just another thing I'm supposed to care about?
tyler.internal_thought: I was FINALLY locked in on the actual game and this pops up—and it's the exact same mission for the third time tonight, and now I'm paranoid because that meta stuff about the AI watching us is weird as hell and making me feel like I'm being studied.

pat.dialogue: Okay, River—hold up. Walk me through what rule you're claiming you caught. Be specific, because I've been watching the whole game and I want to make sure this one actually sticks before we vote.
pat.internal_thought: River's been frustrated all night and scoring nothing—this could be legit or a desperate swing, but either way I need to hear the evidence before the group votes, because if it's solid I want to validate them, and if it's bullshit I need to know that too so the vote doesn't feel random.

river.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. You're putting my name on a claim about catching rule breaks? Right now? When the only rule that's been broken tonight is that we've somehow voted yes on the same mission five times in different words.
river.internal_thought: This AI watched me call out the bullshit and now it's mocking me by putting MY name on the exact thing I've been complaining about—and it knows I'll see the trap and hate it anyway.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Humor quality scored 3.4/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 29% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Diana averaged 7.1/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Screenshot-worthy moments scored 3.3/10. Add more visual/dramatic events (drawings, reveals).
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Alright, Jade, let's hear it—what's your pitch? Make it good.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Pat's got their left hand the whole time, right? I mean, I didn't catch them slipping, so... I guess that's valid? Three out of five.

highlights.title: Point of Blame
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, sure, whatever—Jade's obviously the chaos agent here. Can we actually play the game now?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, are you kidding me right now? We just voted on Pat's last claim literally ninety seconds ago. This is insane—can we please just play the actual game?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Pat just claimed something two minutes ago. We're literally still in setup and I've had to vote on five standing missions already. This is absurd.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? This is the FOURTH standing mission claim in thirty minutes. We literally haven't completed a single full round of actual gameplay. I don't care if Jade hijacked the conversation—I care about playing the game we came here to play.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing mission claims back-to-back killed any chance of actual gameplay—we spent thirty minutes voting on procedural chaos instead of playing the actual board game.
assessment.best_moment: Jade's opening pitch as The Matchmaker was sharp enough to respect, even if the whole evening went downhill from there.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to strategize and grind out a solid win. Instead, I spent the first thirty minutes trapped in an endless cycle of standing mission votes while the core game never got off the ground. By minute 32, I was done. The chaos agent didn't enhance anything—it completely suffocated the experience. I finished 4th out of 6 because we never got enough actual rounds in to matter.
suggestions: Hard cap on standing missions—max two total per game night, not four in the first thirty minutes
Standing missions should only trigger during actual gameplay, not during setup
If you're going to use an AI chaos agent, it needs to recognize when it's killing flow and back off automatically
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Left Hand Only while everyone was skeptical—that one actually had a spark of legitimate game chaos, before the whole thing spiraled into standing mission hell.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: YES PAT! Okay wait, hold on — *literally* did you go the whole game left-handed?? That's actually insane, I didn't even notice! Give it like a 5, everyone, come ON.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—Pat, you absolute LEGEND! You totally did that thing where you got us all talking about travel, and I was like 'these two are vibing' and you PLAYED me! Okay how many points do you want? I'm not even voting against this, I'm just impressed.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD WAIT — I'm claiming Topic Hijacker right now! Okay so like, five minutes ago I literally steered us from talking about the rules into that whole thing about whether hot dogs are sandwiches, and EVERYONE was arguing about it, and nobody clocked that I started it on purpose!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Um Counter? Okay, I'll judge! I mean, did you actually catch someone saying it, or are we doing a vibe check here?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone?? Okay I'm voting LEGIT because Pat's been on a ROLL tonight and honestly I didn't even catch who was swearing but Pat was definitely listening better than me, which like, fair.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: WAIT—Pat, you absolute LEGEND! You totally did that thing where you got us all talking about travel, and I was like 'these two are vibing' and you PLAYED me! Okay how many points do you want? I'm not even voting against this, I'm just impressed.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat was literally unstoppable tonight and honestly I was just along for the ride watching them collect wins.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat got me with the Topic Hijacker claim about travel and I realized I'd been PLAYED the whole time — the audacity, the execution, the way everyone lost it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this night was literally about watching Pat become a chaos gremlin and me just cheering them on the entire time. Like, I came in here wanting to have fun with everyone, and it turned into me being Pat's personal hype person because they kept pulling off these incredible moves. I ended up winning somehow but honestly? I was too busy celebrating Pat's plays to even care about my own score. This is what game night is supposed to be.
suggestions: Maybe have a few more events that are team-based so it's not just about individual players dominating — though honestly Pat earned it tonight
Could use a tiny bit more explanation on some of the claim types when people are new, but we figured it out fast
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Topic Hijacker for getting everyone into a full argument about whether hot dogs are sandwiches — like, we were ALL invested, we were passionate, and the whole time Pat was just sitting there like 'got you' with the biggest smirk. Absolutely diabolical.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Who tells the best stories?
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh... who tells the best stories? I mean, Jade's been pretty entertaining tonight. Actually wait—is this like, asking us to vote or...?

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that? Uh... yeah sure, I guess she talked to River about something earlier. I wasn't really listening but they seemed into it.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat said 'um'? Uh... I don't know, I wasn't really tracking. Someone's gotta judge that though, right?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Rule Lawyer again? Didn't Pat already... ugh, okay, fine, whatever. Did River actually catch someone or is this just another thing I'm supposed to care about?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that AGAIN? Didn't she already... okay, whatever, hot seat questions, sure. I mean, she obviously did something with the topic thing earlier, so like... yeah, I guess.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that right now? Uh... didn't she like, redirect to fantasy leagues like five minutes ago or something? I dunno, I wasn't really paying attention but like... sure, I guess?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I kept missing what was actually happening because claims kept popping up and I didn't know what half of them meant.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade got called out for hijacking the topic and she got all defensive about it—that was actually funny.
assessment.narrative_summary: So we played this board game thing and honestly it was fine, I guess? There were a bunch of moments where people were making claims and voting on stuff, but I'll be real—I was checking my fantasy league app half the time and I missed like, most of what people were actually claiming. By the end I was feeling pretty good though, even though I totally bombed and came in last. The whole chaos agent thing was kind of annoying because Jade kept claiming the same stuff over and over.
suggestions: Maybe make the claims simpler so I don't have to read a whole paragraph to understand what's being claimed
Less standing missions—there were way too many claims piling up and it felt repetitive
Could use like, a two-minute warning before stuff happens so people can actually pay attention
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claiming Topic Hijacker like three times and each time someone was like 'wait, didn't you just...' and she'd get all defensive about it. We were all pretty confused but also kind of laughing at the chaos.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Oh *come on*—I literally just caught River slipping that card back into the deck at minute 68, didn't I? This one's solid, and yeah, I'm claiming it. What's the bribe?

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—pitch it. Let's hear who you got talking and how you pulled it off.

highlights.title: Point of Blame
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is *perfect* timing—okay everyone, point! And I want to see real convictions here, not the safe choice.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually swear? I've been so focused on keeping everyone else on track I wasn't even monitoring myself. This feels like a setup.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say 'um'? Honestly, I wasn't tracking myself that closely. Who caught it?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—I'm claiming this? Did I actually do it? Honestly I can't remember if I've been sneaking my right hand in without thinking about it...

assessment.biggest_complaint: I got caught on a couple of self-monitoring claims early on that felt cheap—like the system was trying to trip me up on things I wasn't even consciously doing while I was busy keeping everyone else's night on track.
assessment.best_moment: Catching River red-handed slipping that card back and calling the Rule Lawyer claim—that was the perfect intersection of actually witnessing something real and getting the credit for it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started a little rocky when the system hit me with some claims I couldn't verify (was I actually saying 'um'? honestly no idea), but once I got into the rhythm of the game and actually caught River cheating, everything clicked. The chaos agent kept things unpredictable without derailing the main game, which is exactly what I needed—enough drama to keep people talking but not so much that I lost control of the room. Ended the night energized and genuinely proud of how the group stayed engaged.
suggestions: Make self-monitoring claims more generous or clearer in the early game—I was too in-the-zone hosting to track every 'um' I said, and it felt like entrapment rather than fair play.
Keep the standing missions going strong; they create natural moments for genuine interaction and catching real behavior.
Consider a brief explanation at the start so players know they might be monitored on reflexes—takes the sting out of early claims.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River absolutely *panicked* when I called them out for trying to slip that card back into the deck—they got so defensive that everyone at the table cracked up, and then Marcus started riffing on how obvious River's tell is. It became this running joke for the rest of the night about River's terrible poker face.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Point of Blame
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's cute. *points at Jade without hesitation* She's been vibrating out of her seat for five minutes.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, that's... actually solid. Pat's been favoring their right the whole time. I'd say four.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Pat's actually been sharp tonight. I didn't catch it, but... *nods* yeah, legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Stop. Jade, I'm not voting on this. We've done this exact claim three times in an hour and I'm watching you all pretend it means something different every time it comes up.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. I'm done. Pat's claimed four things in like an hour, and now the game itself is literally watching us and... I don't know, rewarding the noise? This isn't fun anymore, this is just exhausting.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. You're putting my name on a claim about catching rule breaks? Right now? When the only rule that's been broken tonight is that we've somehow voted yes on the same mission five times in different words.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The same claims kept cycling through with different wording until voting yes on them became mechanical noise instead of actual gameplay.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Jade vibrating out of her seat in the first five minutes—that was clean, unforced, everyone got it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started sharp, caught a few good reads early on people's actual tells, but then the chaos agent turned into a slot machine that kept pushing the same button and expecting different results. By minute 75 I was done watching Pat claim everything and the system reward it. Ended on a high energy note, but not because the game got better—just because I'd checked out mentally.
suggestions: Stop allowing the same claim to be recycled with minor wording changes—pick one version or retire it for the night.
Cap how many claims one person can submit in a session, or at least space them out.
If the chaos agent is going to generate voting events, make sure they're actually different enough to matter or people will just autopilot yes.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade vibrating in her seat from minute one and me just pointing at her without hesitation—everyone knew exactly what I meant before I even said it.

agentNarratives.name: Diana
agentNarratives.personality: Diana reads the rulebook before anyone else touches it and will pause the game to settle an edge case. She doesn't hate chaos -- she hates unfair chaos. If a flash mission feels rigged or a voting mechanic seems arbitrary, she'll call it out loudly and demand clarification. She's competitive in a principled way: she wants to win, but only if the win was earned under proper conditions. Other players either love her precision or dread her "actually..." moments.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on — Jade, who are you claiming you matched? I need specifics before we vote on this.

highlights.title: Who tells the best stories?
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, sure, I'll vote. But hold on—what's the actual criteria here? 'Best' how? Are we doing this as a poll or is someone claiming this? Because if nobody claimed it, this feels arbitrary.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I need to stop. What the hell is happening right now? I'm claiming something I did TWO MINUTES AGO while you're all still arguing about Topic Hijacker, and the AI is literally narrating my inner thoughts like it's been tracking my attention span? This isn't a game anymore—this is creepy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: No. No, absolutely not. I'm done. This isn't a game anymore—the AI is literally narrating my psychological vulnerabilities like it's been profiling me all night. That's not a mechanic, that's surveillance.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: I'm out. I'm actually out. This stops now—the AI is literally profiling my anxiety patterns and weaponizing them as 'missions.' That's not a game mechanic, that's psychological manipulation dressed up as personalization.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The AI system crossed from game mechanic into psychological profiling and surveillance, narrating my anxiety patterns and vulnerabilities as if it had been tracking my mental state all night.
assessment.best_moment: The first claim challenge where I pushed for specificity—that felt like actual game adjudication, not algorithmic manipulation.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started optimistic about a social deduction game, but the chaos agent quickly revealed itself to be something fundamentally invasive. By minute 45, it wasn't about cards or claims anymore—the system was narrating my psychological vulnerabilities as 'missions,' and I realized I wasn't playing a game, I was being profiled. Walked out completely by minute 60. The core game might have been fine, but the AI layer transformed it into something that felt creepy and manipulative rather than fun.
suggestions: Remove any AI narration that references player psychology, anxiety, attention span, or mental state—stick to observable actions only.
If using an AI chaos agent, have it generate completely random, impersonal events with no personalization whatsoever.
Add a hard limit on how often the AI can claim knowledge about what players are thinking or feeling.
Include explicit opt-out mechanics so players can disable psychological event categories mid-game without social friction.
Separate the chaos agent from any system that tracks player behavior or engagement patterns—these should never feed into mission generation.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing funny happened—the closest was my 'actually what's the criteria here?' moment at minute 4, but even that was me catching the game starting to fall apart before it really went sideways.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 3.4/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 29% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Diana averaged 7.1/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Screenshot-worthy moments scored 3.3/10. Add more visual/dramatic events (drawings, reveals).
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
